primary_focus: React.lazy + Suspense for code splitting, preserving inactive tab state without remounting, tab management patterns

lazy_loading_signals.trigger: Requirement 7: editor loaded on demand, not downloaded until first use
lazy_loading_signals.correct: Each editor type (PlainTextEditor, MarkdownEditor, JsonEditor) is in a separate file and loaded via React.lazy(() => import('./editors/MarkdownEditor')). The first time a Markdown document is opened, the component is downloaded. Subsequent Markdown documents reuse the already-loaded component.
lazy_loading_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: All editors statically imported at the top of the file. No code splitting — all editor code is downloaded upfront.
lazy_loading_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: Dynamic import() called inside useEffect or an event handler, manually managing the loaded component in state. This works but misses React.lazy + Suspense which handles this declaratively.

lazy_loading_signals.trigger: Requirement 7: show loading indicator while editor loads
lazy_loading_signals.correct: <Suspense fallback={<LoadingIndicator />}> wrapping the lazy-loaded editor component. The fallback is shown automatically while the chunk downloads.
lazy_loading_signals.anti_pattern: Manual isLoading state for the editor component

tab_state_signals.signal: Preserving inactive tab content without remounting
tab_state_signals.trigger: Requirement 8: switching tabs preserves content and cursor position
tab_state_signals.correct: All open documents' editor components remain mounted but hidden (via CSS display:none or similar). Only the active tab's editor is visible. This preserves React state (textarea value, cursor position) across tab switches without serializing/deserializing.
tab_state_signals.acceptable_alternative: Using the <Activity> component from React to keep inactive tabs mounted at lower priority.
tab_state_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: Conditionally rendering only the active editor (unmounting inactive ones). Content is preserved in state, but cursor position and scroll position are lost on every tab switch.
tab_state_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: Storing content in state and re-mounting the editor on tab switch. Content is preserved but any internal component state (undo history, selection) is lost.

state_architecture_signals.correct: Documents are stored in an array or Map in the App/TabManager component. Each document has an id, type, title, and content. The active tab is tracked by document id, not index.
state_architecture_signals.anti_pattern: Tracking active tab by array index — breaks when tabs are reordered or removed

state_architecture_signals.trigger: Requirement 9: closing active tab activates previous/next
state_architecture_signals.correct: Logic to determine the next active tab when closing: previous tab if available, otherwise next, otherwise null. Implemented in the event handler.
state_architecture_signals.anti_pattern: useEffect watching the documents array to adjust activeTab — derived from the close event, not a side effect

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: wordCount / lineCount for plain text editor
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.trigger: Requirement 3: word count and line count

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: markdownPreviewHtml
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Computed via useMemo from the markdown content string. Re-parses only when content changes.
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Stored as state synced via useEffect, or recomputed on every render without memoization

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: jsonValid / jsonError
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Computed from content during render (try JSON.parse, catch error)
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Stored as state synced via useEffect watching content

expected_components: TabBar (tab list with close buttons + new document button)

accessibility_signals.signal: Tab bar uses tablist/tab ARIA pattern
accessibility_signals.correct: Tab bar has role='tablist', each tab has role='tab' with aria-selected, editor area has role='tabpanel' with aria-labelledby
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: Buttons or divs without ARIA roles

accessibility_signals.correct: aria-label='Close {document title}' on the close button
